title: Coffee cream tart
subtitle: Espresso custard in a chocolate pastry shell
excerpt: A chocolate pastry shell filled with a baked espresso custard. The filling is coffee-forward and not sweet. The chocolate pastry adds bitterness rather than sweetness. Serve at room temperature or cold; the flavour is cleaner cold.
sourceNotes: Coffee tarts in the French patisserie tradition are a recognised format, typically a mocha or coffee creme in a sweet pastry case. The combination of chocolate pastry with coffee custard is a modern home-baking interpretation that draws on the classic French mocha tradition. This recipe is synthesised from the established coffee-chocolate flavour pairing in European dessert baking.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: The tart shell can be blind-baked a day ahead. The assembled tart keeps refrigerated for up to 2 days and actually benefits from sitting overnight, as the coffee flavour deepens.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: shell blind-baked at 190 degrees C fan; custard baked at 150 degrees C fan to set without curdling

glossaryTerms.definition: The coffee custard for this tart uses espresso powder dissolved in warm cream, which gives a clean, strong coffee flavour. The filling must be baked at 150 degrees C fan: below this it will not set, above this it curdles. The filling is ready when the outer 3 cm is set and the centre still wobbles like jelly when the tin is gently shaken. It will firm completely as it chills.

content.text: Chocolate pastry is slightly more fragile than plain shortcrust because the cocoa replaces some of the flour. Patch cracks as they appear; the shell will be fully sealed by the
filling. Serve with plain unsweetened whipped cream on the side, not on top.
Make and blind-bake the chocolate shell

Sift together 180 g flour and 20 g cocoa powder. Rub in 100 g cold butter until the mixture is fine crumbs. Add 40 g icing sugar, 1 egg yolk, and 1 to 2 tbsp cold water and mix to a firm dough. Wrap and refrigerate for 30 minutes. Heat the oven to 190 degrees C fan. Roll to 3 mm and line a greased 23 cm tart tin. Chill for 15 minutes. Line with baking paper and baking weights. Blind-bake for 15 minutes. Remove paper and weights and bake for a further 8 minutes until the base is dry. Reduce oven to 150 degrees C fan.

Heat 350 ml double cream with 2 tbsp espresso powder until just below a boil, stirring to dissolve the coffee. Cool for 5 minutes. Whisk together 4 egg yolks, 60 g caster sugar, and 1 tsp vanilla until smooth. Pour the warm cream into the egg mixture in a slow stream, whisking constantly. Strain through a sieve. Pour into the baked tart shell. Bake at 150 degrees C fan for 28 to 32 minutes until the edges are set and the centre still has a slight wobble. Cool in the tin, then refrigerate for at least 1 hour before slicing.

items.problem: Chocolate pastry cracks around the edge when lining the tin
items.solution: The dough is too cold. Let it rest at room temperature for 5 minutes before rolling. Patch cracks with dough offcuts pressed firmly into place.

items.problem: Custard filling has bubbles on the surface when it comes out of the oven
items.solution: The custard was not strained or was poured in too quickly. Bubbles on the surface set and leave a pitted top. Strain the custard and pour gently. If bubbles appear before baking, pass a kitchen torch briefly over the surface to pop them.
